title: The fly-away horse by Eugene Field — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
Oh, a wonderful horse is the Fly-Away Horse -
Perhaps you have seen him before;
Perhaps, while you slept, his shadow has swept

Through the moonlight that floats on the floor.
For it's only at night, when the stars twinkle bright,
That the Fly-Away Horse, with a neigh
And a pull at his rein and a toss of his mane,
meaning: "The fly-away horse" by Eugene Field is a sad and deep and nature and inspirational poem consisting of 60 lines. The poet explores themes of sad and deep and nature and inspirational through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "Oh, a wonderful horse is the Fly-Away Horse - Perhaps you have seen him before;...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Eugene Field's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
